title: Iran sanctions surpass 1,000 designations since Feb 2025 with shadow-banking focus
summary: Sanctions Watch documented OFAC's latest Iran action that brings Iran-related designations past 1,000 since February 2025, with the new tranche targeting 35 shadow-banking facilitators, 40 dark-fleet shipping firms, a Chinese refinery and 14 weapons-procurement nodes.
contents:
# Iran sanctions cross 1,000-designation threshold with shadow-banking focus

## Cumulative Tally
Sanctions Watch tracking confirms that OFAC's Tuesday action pushes the cumulative count of Iran-related designations past 1,000 since the campaign accelerated in February 2025. The new tranche names 35 entities and individuals operating Iran's shadow-banking corridors, 40 shipping firms tied to Iran's dark fleet, an unnamed Chinese refinery accused of receiving sanctioned crude, and 14 procurement actors moving Shahed-series drone components and missile parts.

## Multi-Jurisdictional Pattern
The designations follow Sanctions Watch's documented pattern of US-led action without parallel EU or UK measures on Iran during the same week, although European authorities continue separate enforcement on Russia. Compliance officers face fresh secondary-sanctions risk on transactions touching the named Chinese refinery, the largest mainland-based downstream actor named in any Iran-related action this year.

## Evasion Tactics Catalogued
Reporting describes ship-to-ship transfers in Malaysian and Singaporean waters, AIS manipulation by dark-fleet tankers, and trade-based money-laundering through UAE and Hong Kong front companies. The shadow-banking layer relies on USDT settlement, parallel-market hawala, and crypto-to-crypto laundering routes.

## Tags
sanctions-watch, ofac, iran, shadow-banking, shadow-fleet, designations, secondary-sanctions, compliance
